title: Machine learning-guided engineering of genetically encoded fluorescent calcium indicators
data_sources: Chen et al. 2013 GCaMP6 mutant library
Dana et al. 2019 jGCaMP7 mutant library
in silico single-point saturation library (1,423 variants)
core_conclusion: A stacked ensemble of three regression models trained on 1,078 GCaMP mutants and encoded with biophysical amino acid properties screened 1,423 novel variants in silico, identifying eGCaMP, eGCaMP+, and eGCaMP2+. The best variant, eGCaMP2+, achieves a 2.5-fold larger fluorescence response than prior fast indicators and outperforms sixth- through eighth-generation GCaMPs, demonstrating that small, curated sequence-function libraries can drive efficient protein engineering when paired with ensemble ML and domain-principled feature encoding.
why_it_matters: Compresses protein engineering from exhaustive trial-and-error to 17 prioritized in-vitro tests; delivers practically superior biosensors validated in primary neurons and live mice; pipeline is structure-agnostic and transferable to any GEFI with a quantifiable readout; validates ensemble learning on small (~1,000 sample) biological datasets; fully open data, code, and reagents.
why_nature: Computational method is the primary discovery engine, not post-hoc analysis; generalizable framework across protein engineering domains; quantitative demonstration of ensemble superiority over individual models; FAIR-compliant open deposition of all materials.
added_value_to_my_research: AAindex domain-principled encoding template: replace one-hot urban feature encoding with built-environment property matrices for 2SFCA and accessibility models
Iterative active-learning loop (propose-test-retrain) mirrors geospatial foundation model fine-tuning with scarce labeled data
Residue contact graph to GNN: analogous to spatial network graph for physics-informed accessibility operators
Ensemble superiority demonstration on small datasets: evidence for ensemble strategies in data-sparse GeoAI health geography settings
SHAP residue-importance ranking: cross-domain template for spatial feature attribution and GWR/MGWR cluster visualization
